metadata.summary: Python SDK and runtime for Flyde - a visual flow-based programming language and IDE.

documentation: # PyFlyde ![Build](https://github.com/trustmaster/pyflyde/actions/workflows/python-package.yml/badge.svg) [](https://pyflyde.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) Python runtime for [Flyde](https://github.com/flydelabs/flyde) with Data Engineering emphasis. ![Example graph running K-means clustering with Pandas and Scikit-learn](https://github.com/trustmaster/pyflyde/blob/main/clustering_example.png?raw=true) Links: - [Flyde homepage](https://flyde.dev) - [Documentation](https://pyflyde.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) ## Getting started You need Python 3.9+ installed on your machine to run PyFlyde. Then you can install PyFlyde to your environment using pip: ```bash pip install pyflyde ``` On some systems Python3 requires `pip3` command instead: ```bash pip3 install pyflyde ``` ### Running the examples You can copy `examples` folder from this repository to your local project to give it a try. Then you can run the example flow with: ```bash pyflyde examples/HelloWorld.flyde ``` Or you can install this package locally after cloning this repository and run the examples directly: ```bash pip install . ./pyflyde examples/HelloWorld.flyde ``` To run other examples you would need to install their dependencies: ```bash pip install examples/ ``` Then you can run e.g. ```bash ./pyflyde examples/Clustering.flyde ``` ### Using the visual editor Install Flyde VSCode extension from the [marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=flyde.flyde-vscode). It will open existing `.flyde` files in the visual editor. You can call `Flyde: New Visual Flow` command in VSCode to create a new flow file. You can browse the component library in the panel on the right. To see your local components click the "View all" button. They will appear under the "Current project". Note that PyFlyde doesn't implement all of the Flyde's stdlib components, only a few essential ones. Whenever you change your component library classes or their interfaces, use `pyflyde gen` command to generate `.flyde.ts` definitions, e.g.: ```bash pyflyde gen examples/mylib/components.py ``` Flyde editor needs `.flyde.ts` files in order to "see" your components. ### Running a Machine Learning example and creating your first project For further details please check [Quickstart](https://pyflyde.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart/) guide. ## Contributing ### Install dev dependencies ```bash pip install .\[dev\] ``` ### Run tests, linters and coverage reports To run tests only: ```bash make test ``` To run tests with coverage and see report: ```bash make cover report ``` To run linters: ```bash make lint ```

classes.name: flyde.stdlib.InlineValue

methods.name: flyde.stdlib.InlineValue._is_inline_dict
documentation: Check if a value is an inline Flyde value dict, which has `type` and `value` keys.

methods.name: flyde.stdlib.InlineValue._get_inline_value
documentation: Get the value from an inline Flyde value output.
InlineValue sends a constant value to output.

classes.name: flyde.stdlib._ConditionType
documentation: Condition type enumeration.

classes.name: flyde.stdlib._ConditionalConfig
documentation: Conditional configuration.

classes.name: flyde.stdlib.Conditional
documentation: Conditional component evaluates a condition against the input and sends the result to output.

classes.name: flyde.stdlib.GetAttribute
documentation: Get an attribute from an object or dictionary.

modules.name: flyde.cli
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python3

functions.name: flyde.cli.gen
documentation: Generate TypeScript files for a module.

functions.name: flyde.io.is_EOF
documentation: Checks if a value is an EOF signal.

variables.name: flyde.io.EOF
documentation: EOF is a signal to indicate the end of data.

classes.name: flyde.io.InputMode
documentation:
InputMode is the mode of an input.

QUEUE: The input is connected to a queue. On each node invocation, a new value is taken from the queue.
If the queue is empty, the node invocation is blocked.
STICKY: The input has a sticky value. It has a queue attached to it, but the last received value is returned in
absence of new values in the queue. Thus sticky inputs are non-blocking.
STATIC: The input has a static value that does not change.

classes.name: flyde.io.Requiredness
documentation:
Requiredness of an input.

REQUIRED: The input is required to be connected.
OPTIONAL: The input is optional.
REQUIRED_IF_CONNECTED: The input is required if it is connected to a queue.

classes.name: flyde.io.OutputMode
documentation:
OutputMode defines the behavior of an output if it is connected to multiple input queues.

REF: Copy-by-reference. Each connected input will receive the same object.
VALUE: Copy-by-value. Each connected input will receive a deep copy of the object.
CIRCLE: Circular. Each connected input will receive the object in a round-robin fashion.

classes.name: flyde.io.Input

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.__init__
documentation:
Create a new input object.

Args:
    id (str): The ID of the input
    description (str): The description of the input
    mode (InputMode): The mode of the input
    typ (type): The type of the input
    value (Any): The value of the input for InputMode = InputMode.STATIC or InputMode = InputMode.STICKY
    required (Required): The requiredness of the input

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.queue
documentation: Get the queue of the input.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.is_connected
documentation: Check if the input is connected to a queue.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.value
documentation: Get the static value associated with the input.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.value
documentation: Set the static value of the input.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.get
documentation: Get the value of the input from either the queue or static value.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.empty
documentation: Check if the input queue is empty.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.count
documentation: Get the number of elements in the input queue.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.inc_ref_count
documentation: Increment the reference count of the input.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.dec_ref_count
documentation: Decrement the reference count of the input.

methods.name: flyde.io.Input.ref_count
documentation: Get the reference count of the input.
Input is an interface for getting input/output data for a node.

classes.name: flyde.io.Output

methods.name: flyde.io.Output.__init__
documentation:
Create a new output object.

Args:
    id (str): The ID of the output
    description (str): The description of the output
    type (type): The type of the output
    delayed (bool): If the output is delayed [not implemented yet]

methods.name: flyde.io.Output.connect
documentation:
Connect a queue to the output.

This method can be called multiple times to connect multiple queues to the same output.

methods.name: flyde.io.Output.connected
documentation: Check if the output is connected to a queue.

methods.name: flyde.io.Output.send
documentation: Put a value in the output queue.
Output is an interface for setting output data for a component.

classes.name: flyde.io.RedirectQueue
documentation:
RedriveQueue is a fake write-only queue that is used by GraphPort
to redrive input values to the output queues.

classes.name: flyde.io.GraphPort
documentation:
GraphPort is an interface between inside and outside of the graph used for input/output.

It combines Input and Output, because Graph Input acts as an Input for outside world,
but outputs values inside the graph. Similarly, Graph Output acts as an Output for outside world,
but receives values from inside the graph.

classes.name: flyde.io.ConnectionNode
documentation:
ConnectionNode is a combination of a node and an input/output pin.

It is used as a source or destination of a connection.

classes.name: flyde.io.Connection

methods.name: flyde.io.Connection.from_yaml
documentation: Create a connection from a parsed YAML dictionary.
Connection is a connection between two nodes in a graph.

classes.name: flyde.flow.Flow

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.factory
documentation:
Factory method to create a node from a class name and arguments.

It is used by the runtime to create nodes from the YAML definition or on the fly.

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.run
documentation: Start the flow running. This is a non-blocking call as the flow runs in a separate thread.

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.run_sync
documentation: Run the flow synchronously. Shutdown handlers will be executed after the flow has finished.

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.node
documentation: The root node of the flow.

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.stopped
documentation: Stopped event is set when the flow has finished working.

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.from_yaml
documentation: Load Flyde Flow definition from parsed YAML dict.

methods.name: flyde.flow.Flow.from_file
documentation: Load Flyde Flow definition from a *.flyde YAML file.
Flow is a root-level runnable directed acyclic graph of nodes.

functions.name: flyde.node.create_instance_id
documentation: Create a unique instance ID.

classes.name: flyde.node.Node

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.run
documentation: Run the node. This method should be overridden by subclasses.

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.stop
documentation: Stop the node. This method should be overridden by subclasses.

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.finish
documentation: Finish the component execution gracefully by closing all its outputs and notifying others.

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.shutdown
documentation: Shutdown the component. This method is optional and can be overridden by subclasses.

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.send
documentation: Send a value to an output.

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.receive
documentation: Receive a value from an input.

methods.name: flyde.node.Node.from_yaml
documentation: Create a node from a parsed YAML dictionary.

Node is the main building block of an application.

Attributes:
    id (str): A unique identifier for the node.
    node_type (str): The node type identifier.
    input_config (dict): A dictionary of input pin configurations.
    display_name (str): A human-readable name for the node.
    inputs (dict[str, Input]): Node input map.
    outputs (dict[str, Output]): Node output map.

classes.name: flyde.node.Component

methods.name: flyde.node.Component.stop
documentation: Stop the component execution.

methods.name: flyde.node.Component.to_ts
documentation: Convert the node to a TypeScript definition.
A node that runs a function when executed.

classes.name: flyde.node.Graph

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph._check_pin
documentation: Check if the instance and pin exist.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.run
documentation: Run the graph.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.shutdown
documentation:
Call shutdown handlers on all instances.

This method is called from the main thread to allow cleanup and things like UI.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.stop
documentation: Stop all instances gracefully.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.terminate
documentation: Terminate all instances immediately.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.stopped
documentation: Return the stopped event which is set when the node has stopped.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.stopped
documentation: Set the stopped event.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.from_yaml
documentation: Create a Graph node from a parsed YAML dictionary.

methods.name: flyde.node.Graph.to_dict
documentation: Return a dictionary representation of the node.
A visual graph node that contains other nodes.
